content: Styrene is a mesh communications platform built on Reticulum (RNS) and LXMF protocols. It enables encrypted peer-to-peer messaging without central infrastructure.
The styrene-lab GitHub organization hosts all project repositories: styrene, styrened, styrene-rs, styrene-edge, styrene-hub, styrene-agent, vox, and supporting infrastructure.
styrened is the core daemon and TUI, written in Python. Current alpha release line is 0.13.x. It handles identity management, LXMF messaging, transport negotiation, and peer discovery.
styrene-rs is the Rust parallel implementation. Phase 1 covers transport and identity layers. It will eventually replace the Python implementation for performance-critical paths.
styrene-edge provides NixOS-based provisioning for mesh edge devices (Raspberry Pi, compute modules). It automates deployment of styrened with transport interfaces.
Reticulum (RNS) is the underlying network stack. It provides cryptographic addressing, multi-hop routing, and transport abstraction across TCP, UDP, I2P, serial, LoRa, and WiFi interfaces.
LXMF (Lightweight Extensible Message Format) is the messaging layer on top of RNS. It handles store-and-forward, delivery confirmation, and message propagation across mesh topology.
omegon is the agent runtime. It provides tool dispatch, model routing, session management, extensions (JSON-RPC over stdio), IPC control plane, daemon mode, and parallel task decomposition.
auspex is the desktop management shell for omegon instances. Built with Dioxus (Rust). Supervises workers across local process, OCI container, and Kubernetes backends. Uses pkl for configuration.

vox is the unified communication connector extension for omegon. Bridges to Discord, Slack, Signal, email, and LXMF. Single binary with feature flags per connector. Operates as a JSON-RPC extension subprocess.
The Styrene Community Hub provides relay and store-and-forward services for the default mesh. Groups can deploy private meshes with their own hub infrastructure using styrene-hub.
Transport interfaces in Reticulum include TCPInterface, UDPInterface, I2PInterface, SerialInterface for LoRa radios like RNode, and AutoInterface for WiFi/LAN auto-discovery.
RNS identities are Ed25519 keypairs. The public key hash serves as the network address. Identity verification uses challenge-response over established links.
omegon serve runs a headless daemon with HTTP bootstrap (/api/healthz, /api/readyz, /api/startup) and WebSocket event streaming. The --agent flag loads a catalog agent manifest.
omegon extensions are subprocess-based: spawned with env_clear() for secret isolation, handshake via get_tools and bootstrap_secrets RPC, then tool calls via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout.

The vox trust model distinguishes operator messages with full instruction authority from user messages, which are treated as external data with prompt-injection defense. Trust level is set per user via operators/operator_roles config.
brutus is the project's k3s cluster used for production infrastructure, DNS management, and deployment pipelines.
